titleStmt.title: Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 5977

div.p: To all Christ's faithful to whom the present written document shall come, Patrick son of Elias Dunning of Renton sends greeting. Know me all of you to have quitclaimed to the house of Coldingham for myself and my heirs in perpetuity the homage and service that Henry my brother and his heirs owe me for one toft and one croft of one acre in the toun of Renton, and two acres in the plain of the same toun, that is, one acre on the west side of 'Cromstaniside', and a large acre above 'Aletone', with all escheats that could be due to me an dmy heirs for the said land. The said Henry and his heirs shall each year give as ferme to the house of Coldingham six pence at Easter; and they shall give twelve pence for merchet, twelve pence for heriot, twelve pence for forfeit. And as witness of this my quitclaim I have applied my seal to this written document. As witness, the lord William of Mordington, the lord Alan of Swinton, Adam of Prenderguest, Robert, Andrew, Alexander of Paxton, Adam of Reston, Bertram his son, Maurice of Ayton, Robert son of Reginald, Ralph the provost, Roger his son, Robert son of Gregory, John son of Elias of Ayton, and others.
